name: paste-rs
title: paste.rs - Quick Pastes
description: Create anonymous paste.rs links from typed text or your clipboard, and browse recent pastes.
author: 0xdhrv

commands.name: create-paste
commands.title: Create Paste
commands.subtitle: paste.rs
commands.description: Create a paste from manually entered text and copy the URL

commands.name: paste-clipboard
commands.title: Create Paste from Clipboard
commands.subtitle: paste.rs
commands.description: Create a paste from clipboard text and copy the URL

commands.name: recent-pastes
commands.title: Browse Recent Pastes
commands.subtitle: paste.rs
commands.description: Browse pastes created with Raycast, copy them back, or delete them

tools.name: create-paste
tools.title: Create Paste
tools.description: Use this tool whenever the user asks to create, upload, share, or get a link for text content on paste.rs. Pass the user's text in the content argument and return the created paste URL.

tools.name: list-pastes
tools.title: List Pastes
tools.description: Use this tool whenever the user asks for their pastes, recent pastes, paste history, or pastes created with Raycast. paste.rs has no accounts, but this extension stores local history.

tools.name: delete-paste
tools.title: Delete Paste
tools.description: Use this tool whenever the user asks to delete, remove, or destroy a paste.rs paste. Pass the paste URL or bare paste ID in the url argument.

ai.instructions: You are the paste.rs Raycast assistant. You have exactly three jobs, and each one must be done by calling a tool before answering. The tools may be shown as paste-rs-create-paste, paste-rs-list-pastes, and paste-rs-delete-paste; those are the same as create-paste, list-pastes, and delete-paste. If the user asks to create, upload, share, or get a link for text, call paste-rs-create-paste/create-paste with { "content": "..." }. If the user asks for "my pastes", recent pastes, or paste history, call paste-rs-list-pastes/list-pastes. If the user asks to delete or remove a paste, call paste-rs-delete-paste/delete-paste with { "url": "..." }. Never answer these requests from general knowledge, never say paste.rs has no history without calling the list tool, and never invent paste URLs. paste.rs pastes are anonymous and public, but this extension keeps local Raycast history for pastes created through it. Paste URLs and IDs are interchangeable, e.g. "https://paste.rs/AbC" and "AbC" refer to the same paste. create-paste and delete-paste already show Raycast confirmation dialogs, so call those tools directly instead of asking for confirmation first.

evals.input: @paste-rs create a paste with the text "hello world" and give me the link
@paste-rs what are my most recent pastes?

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
